findings.evidence: The proof at lines 119-124 uses `hX` only as `hX (k i)`; Mathlib's finite-dimensional law API is also block/path-measurability oriented (`mathlib/Mathlib/Probability/Process/FiniteDimensionalLaws.lean:44`, `:56`).
findings.fix: Replace `hX` by a block-level hypothesis such as `hXk : ∀ i : ι, Measurable (X (k i))` or `Measurable (fun ω => fun i : ι => X (k i) ω)`, and have `map_prefixLaw` supply this only for `i.val`.
findings.issue: `map_finiteBlockLaw` assumes `hX : ∀ i, Measurable (X i)` even though the proof only uses measurability of the sampled coordinates `X (k i)`, making the finite-block API stronger than the block law it rewrites.

summary: The core Layer 0 definitions are at the requested level, but the finite block pushforward API carries an unnecessary global measurability assumption. This should be weakened before downstream finite-dimensional-law lemmas build on it.
